records.ety: [L. circularis, fr. circulus circle: cf. F. circulaire. See Circle.]

defs: In the form of, or bounded by, a circle; round.
repeating itself; ending in itself; reverting to the point of beginning; hence, illogical; inconclusive; as, circular reasoning.
Adhering to a fixed circle of legends; cyclic; hence, mean; inferior. See Cyclic poets, under Cyclic.
quotes: Had Virgil been a circular poet, and closely adhered to history, how could the Romans have had Dido?

defs: Addressed to a circle, or to a number of persons having a common interest; circulated, or intended for circulation; as, a circular letter.
quotes: A proclamation of Henry III., . . . doubtless circular throughout England.
A man so absolute and circular In all those wished-for rarities that may take A virgin captive.

records.ety: [Cf. (for sense 1) F. circulaire, lettre circulaire. See Circular, a.]

defs: A circular letter, or paper, usually printed, copies of which are addressed or given to various persons; as, a business circular.
A sleeveless cloak, cut in circular form.
